channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel says Iran has launched missile attack
item.description: Iran has launched missiles toward Israel, Israeli officials said Tuesday, shortly after the U.S. warned an attack was imminent. Israeli military spokesperson Rear Adm. Daniel Hagari said around 7:30 p.m. local time in Israel that missiles had just been launched from Iran.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Israel says Iran has fired missiles and it warns residents to shelter in place
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Israeli military said Tuesday that Iran has fired missiles and it ordered residents to remain close to bomb shelters as air raid sirens sounded across the country.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: IDF meets little resistance from Hezbollah after weeks of hitting terror targets, officials say
item.description: The Israel Defense Force met little resistance from Hezbollah terrorists despite weeks of growing anticipation of an Israeli incursion amid strikes and raids by specials ops units in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian national indicted in ransomware attacks in US
item.description: A Russian national is accused in a series of ransomware attacks in Texas and other parts of the U.S. over a seven-year period, the Department of Justice (DOJ) announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Iran fires missiles at Israel, IDF says
item.description: Iran has launched a missile attack against Israel, the Israel Defense Force (IDF) said Tuesday, warning residents to shelter in place.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The new head of NATO vowed on Tuesday to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and expressed confidence that he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance's most powerful member, in November.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know as Israel says it has launched ground incursion into Lebanon
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel said its ground troops crossed into Lebanon overnight, launching what the military described as “localized” raids to root out Hezbollah fighters and infrastructure. Hezbollah, meanwhile, said it saw no sign of Israeli forces and its troops were ready to confront them.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A likely Russian artillery strike kills at least 6 at a Ukrainian market
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An apparent Russian artillery strike hit a market in the southern Ukraine city of Kherson on Tuesday, killing at least six people and wounding three others, authorities said, on a morning when Ukrainians across the country were observing a minute’s silence for their military and
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China marks 75 years of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China is marking the 75th year of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger over the massive state.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum to be sworn in as 1st female president of Mexico, a country with pressing problems
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Claudia Sheinbaum will take the oath of office Tuesday as Mexico’s first female president in more than 200 years of independence, promising to protect an expanded social safety net and fight for the poor like her predecessor, but facing pressing problems.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, MEGAN JANETSKY and MARK STEVENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel begins ground offensive against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli ground forces crossed into southern Lebanon early Tuesday, marking a significant escalation of an offensive against Hezbollah militants and opening a new front in a yearlong war against its Iranian-backed adversaries.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, ABBY SEWELL and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Humberto Ortega, Nicaragua's ex-military chief who later turned critic of his brother, dies at 77
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Nicaraguan guerrilla fighter Humberto Ortega, a Sandinista defense minister who later in life became a critic of his older brother President Daniel Ortega, died Monday at 77, Nicaragua's army said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indian-controlled Kashmir votes in final phase of polls to elect local government
item.description: Voting has begun in Indian-controlled Kashmir in the final phase of an election to choose a local government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Live music, breakfast and a raffle: Mexico's president makes the most of his final day in office
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Outgoing President Andrés Manuel López Obrador turned his final morning briefing Monday into a variety show featuring live music, breakfast and a raffle to gift his watch to a journalist. “I dedicated myself to serve the people,” the 70-year-old leader said.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli ministers frustrated over US, IDF leak on Lebanon operation: Report
item.description: Israeli ministers agree to operational strategy for ground invasion hours after officials in the US confirmed a 'limited' invasion of Lebanon was coming, drawing frustration from Jerusalem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japan's new premier vows to push strong defense under Japan-US alliance and boost economy
item.description: The newly elected Japanese prime minister pledged to stick to the vital Japan-U.S. alliance amid growing tension in the region while calling for it to be more equitable.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel launches limited ground operations in Lebanon as war against Hezbollah, terrorist groups continue
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) began limited operations in Lebanon on Monday evening, continuing its battle against Hezbollah terrorists. The military killed several key figures of the group over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Death toll rises to 18 in South Africa mass shootings, police say
item.description: The death toll from mass shootings at two houses on the same street in a South African village over the weekend has risen to 18, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's ground invasion into Lebanon imminent as cabinet approves next phase of the war
item.description: President Biden demanded a 'ceasefire now' following reports that Washington had been briefed by Israel on its plans to launch a 'limited' ground invasion into Lebanon imminently in fight with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Navy pushes to catch up to China’s superiority at sea
item.description: The U.S. Navy is gearing up for a major war in the South China Sea by the end of the decade, reshaping military posture and structure as it aims to catch up to a larger Chinese naval force. Chief of Naval Operations Adm.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: At least 148 are dead in floods and landslides in Nepal
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal (AP) — Rescuers in Nepal recovered dozens of bodies from buses and other vehicles that were buried in landslides near the capital Kathmandu, as the death toll from flooding rose to at least 148 with dozens missing, officials said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BINAJ GURUBACHARYA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says he will talk to Netanyahu as Israel pummels Sunni terror targets in Beirut
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces reportedly began striking the Lebanese capital of Beirut on Sunday evening after President Biden said he intended to speak with Prime Minister Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Britt says she thinks Putin 'respects' Trump
item.description: Sen. Katie Britt (R-Ala.) said in a Sunday interview she thinks Russian President Vladimir Putin “respects” former President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Another senior Hezbollah commander, Ali Karki, confirmed killed in Friday airstrike
item.description: Hezbollah confirmed that another one of its top commanders, Ali Karki, was killed in an Israeli airstrike on Friday. The Iranian-backed Lebanese militant group said Karki was killed alongside the group’s leader, Hassan Nasrallah.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Lithuanian FM warns Russia can do 'so much damage to its neighbors'
item.description: Lithuania has been one of the most vocal opponents of Russia since Moscow's invasion of Ukraine in 2022. The NATO country has raised its defense spending to over 3.2% annually.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IDF announces death of another senior Hezbollah official following Nasrallah death
item.description: The Israeli military says it killed yet another member of Hezbollah's top command on Sunday, after wiping out leadership in a Friday strike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli military says Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah killed in Beirut strike
item.description: The Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) confirmed the leader of the Hezbollah terrorist organization Hassan Nasrallah died in its strike Friday against the group’s headquarters in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reward offered for Iranian in alleged Bolton assassination plot
item.description: Iranian Vice-President Mohammad Javad Zarif rejected accusations that Iran has tried to assassinate government critics overseas.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy meet in New York as election holds high stakes for US support for Ukraine
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met face-to-face with Donald Trump on Friday with public tensions rising between the two over Ukraine's defense against Russia's invasion and in the midst of the U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iranian operatives charged with hacking Trump's presidential campaign
item.description: The operatives are accused of hacking Trump's campaign and disseminating stolen information to media organizations.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu vows to use 'full force' against Hezbollah and dims hopes for a cease-fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday vowed to carry out “full force” strikes against Hezbollah until it ceases firing rockets across the border, dimming hopes for a cease-fire proposal put forth by U.S. and European officials.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, BASSEM MROUE and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Harris blasts proposals for Ukraine to cede territory to Russia during Zelenskyy meeting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With American support for Ukraine at a partisan crossroads, Vice President Kamala Harris on Thursday slammed suggestions that Kyiv should cede territory for the sake of peace with Moscow as “dangerous and unacceptable.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US and allies call for an immediate 21-day cease-fire between Israel and Hezbollah
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S., France and other allies jointly called Wednesday for an immediate 21-day cease-fire to allow for negotiations in the escalating conflict between Israel and Hezbollah that has killed more than 600 people in Lebanon in recent days.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MATTHEW LEE and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel tells its troops to prepare for a possible ground operation in Lebanon
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel is preparing for a possible ground operation in Lebanon, its army chief said Wednesday as Hezbollah fired dozens of rockets across the border and a missile aimed at Tel Aviv that was the militant group's deepest strike yet.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, TIA GOLDENBERG and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China test-fires an intercontinental ballistic missile into the Pacific Ocean
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China test-fired an intercontinental ballistic missile into the Pacific Ocean on Wednesday, stirring security concerns in the region already tense over Beijing's territorial claims and rivalry with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu will address the UN as Israel, bogged down by one war, barrels toward another
item.description: The war in Gaza is about to hit the one-year mark, and Israel is on the cusp of a wider regional war with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel bombards Hezbollah, killing a top commander, while families flee southern Lebanon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel killed a top Hezbollah commander Tuesday as part of a two-day bombing campaign that has left more than 560 people dead and prompted thousands in southern Lebanon to seek refuge from the widening conflict.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation
